tagline: Risk and payments intelligence for regulated B2B financial workflows
product_suite: Risk Scoring (KYC/KYB, fraud signals, underwriting features)
Analytics (cohort, ARPU, fraud rates, payment success)
sales_motion: Enterprise B2B; average deal cycles 4–7 months; ACV $150k–$400k; security and compliance diligence is mandatory in 95% of deals.

security_compliance.soc2: Type II certified (renewal due in Q4)
security_compliance.pci_dss: SAQ A-EP target by Q3 (today: partial controls, not fully attested)
security_compliance.data_residency: US primary, EU region requested by prospects (under evaluation)
security_compliance.third_party_risk: Policy draft exists; not formally approved or operationalized

services.backend: Node/TypeScript monolith with modular boundaries (billing, auth, payments, reporting)
services.ml_scoring: Python microservice for risk scoring (FastAPI) served behind internal gateway
services.data: Postgres (OLTP), S3 data lake, Snowflake for analytics

infra.feature_flags: Homegrown toggles (limited targeting, no kill-switch by cohort)

strategy: Re-architect billing to support clean usage-based pricing by Q3
Launch Payments EU (PSD2-compliant) by end of Q3
Improve reliability: MTTR < 2 hours, Sev1 < 2 per quarter by Q4
Adopt enterprise-grade feature flags to enable safe launches by Q2
Formalize third-party risk management policy and process by end of Q2
Shift to product squads with clear API boundaries and platform teams by end of Q1/Q2
org_design_objectives: Move from functional to product model with 6–8 person squads
Create platform axes (DevEx, Data Platform, Payments Core) to reduce duplication
Establish decision SLAs and escalation path to speed decisions

compliance_non_negotiables: No PII flows without DPIA and DPA updates
Vendor risk review required pre-procurement for all high-risk vendors

known_hotspots.payments: Initiative has grown to 12 engineers working off one backlog; multiple gateways and methods; conflicting priorities US vs EU
known_hotspots.billing: Legacy code in monolith, shared DB tables with orders; hard to decouple
known_hotspots.on_call: SRE primary; application teams not fully owning services; incident roles unclear
known_hotspots.frontend: Shared component library creates cross-team coupling; long PR queues

risks: Regulatory slippage (PCI, SOC2) delaying enterprise deals
Matrix-like behavior if platform and product reporting not clarified
Hiring ahead of managerial capacity leading to burnout and culture drift
notes_for_consultant: We want a concrete 12-month plan to move to product squads with platform boundaries; keep compliance and enterprise expectations central; prefer minimal matrix complexity and clear decision rights.
